name: DiRAC - Data-Intensive (Leicester)
oneLiner: Leicester's DiRAC data-intensive service, built for big-data throughput and AI-adjacent scientific pipelines.
summary: The Leicester DiRAC data-intensive service focuses on high-I/O, data-rich workloads for particle physics, astrophysics, and cosmology. The published programme summary cites 40,288 CPU cores, 177 TB of memory, and 7 PB of storage, emphasising strong throughput for processing very large datasets. That same profile suits AI workflows where large preprocessing jobs and storage bandwidth dominate over accelerator density.

keyFacts.hardware: 40,288 CPU cores, 177 TB memory, and 7 PB storage
keyFacts.energy: Purpose-built institutional data platform within DiRAC

access.route: Projects access the service through DiRAC's central allocation process when aligned with programme priorities.

useCases: Scientific inference pipelines with heavy I/O demands
Mission-specific analytics for astronomy and particle physics
